title: Pelvic pain in a 26-year-old female
presentation: A 26-year-old female presents with 2 days of right lower pelvic pain and one episode of light vaginal bleeding. Her last menstrual period was 7 weeks ago. She has a history of a prior pelvic inflammatory disease. Vitals: BP 100/68, HR 114, RR 18, Temp 37.0C, SpO2 99%. Exam: right adnexal tenderness, cervical motion tenderness, no peritoneal signs yet.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Positive pregnancy test + adnexal tenderness + hemodynamic instability = ruptured ectopic until proven otherwise. OB/GYN consult immediately. Do NOT delay for imaging if unstable.
